facts.text: Old Miakka’s historic Miakka School House, built in 1914, was the first school in Manatee County funded by a state bond in 1913.
On July 3 1986, the Miakka School House earned a spot on the National Register of Historic Places, preserving its early‑20th‑century legacy.
Community members gathered on October 23 to review plans for the Old Miakka Preserve, a project that blends history, nature, and local input.
The area that became Old Miakka was once a sawgrass swamp bounded by Tatum Ridge, Phillippi Creek, and Bee Ridge, shaping its early settlement.
Old Miakka sits within the North Port–Bradenton–Sarasota Metropolitan Statistical Area, linking it to a larger Floridian region.
The Miakka School House’s 1914 construction marked a milestone, as it was the first school in the area to receive state bond funding for its building.
